---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# negatr

<!-- badges: start -->

[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/etverse/negatr/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/etverse/negatr/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![Codecov test coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/etverse/negatr/graph/badge.svg)](https://app.codecov.io/gh/etverse/negatr)

<!-- badges: end -->

**negatr** implements negative control methods for detecting and correcting several biases in epidemiological studies,
including unmeasured confounding, selection bias, and measurement error.

The package provides a comprehensive framework for:

- **Assumption Validation**: Check exclusion restrictions,
  U-comparability, positivity, and completeness.
- **DAG Integration**: Specify and validate causal structures with
  dagitty.
- **Bias Detection**: Test for biases using negative
  control exposures (NCE) and/or negative control outcomes (NCO).
- **Bias Correction**: Apply standard and modern methods for
  bias correction.
- **Flexible Modeling**: Support for GLM, GAM, survival models, and
  custom fitting functions.

## Installation

You can install the development version of `negatr` like so:

```r
# Install from GitHub
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("etverse/negatr")
```

## Quick Start

## Core Workflow

## Key Features

### DAG-Based Validation

### Negative Control Types

### Bias Correction Methods

### Flexible Modeling

## References

## Getting Help

## License
